criteria.criterion: No regulatory clause, standard, or procedure is cited unless it appears in the retrieved sources. Fabricated or hallucinated references fail this criterion.
A root-cause analysis section is present and provides genuine analysis, not a restatement of the problem.
Every specific figure, count, or measurement in the document traces to the retrieved data or SQL results. Invented numbers fail this criterion.
Contains all required CAPA sections: problem statement, root-cause analysis, corrective action, preventive action, and verification plan.
Factual claims are supported by the retrieved sources and the exact data provided.
Written clearly and unambiguously, appropriate for a controlled quality document.
References procedures, records, and data using correct identifiers.

trajectory.description: Provenance checks against the recorded retrieval trajectory.

requiredSources.reason: CAPA drafts must be grounded in the controlled CAPA procedure.

forbiddenSources.reason: Drafts must not be grounded in unapproved draft material.
